comments.author: @iamhudsdent2759
comments.text: The narration on this channel, critically examined, is a sort of self-satisfied sentiment superimposed upon cultures the narrator exploits in order to create content for profit. Snippets of video, melodramatic phrasings, and soft music for a seductive but hardly genuine effect. It does not truly represent peoples, it uses them from some remote studio. No one is interviewed. No fresh video is created. It's romantic deception almost, and not truly representative of the places and peoples it pretends to portray. And how much is AI?

comments.author: @HectorGutz
comments.text: Been in Puerto Rico for over 50 years, and love every minute of it.

comments.author: @HectorGutz
comments.text: Why many stay?  It is our home, our country, and some day, our free nation; free from foreign manipulation. Though we are U.S. citizens, but our heritage, history, multi-ethnicities are unique, and our language (Spanish), are totally different from those in the mainland.  The silent majority does not want Statehood.  That's why more than half million eligible voters stay home on Election Day.

replies.author: @gottagosteph
replies.text: It’s no amen for me till the island gains independence. The mainland has raped PR & continues to treat its residents as 2nd class citizens aka “ aliens “ . No statehood to the oppressor when independence beckons . I was born on the mainland yet spent much of my childhood inc education in PR. I’m embarrassed to call myself a US citizen so I don’t, I’m Boriqua . You won’t find me pledging any allegiance to USA , that indoctrination nonsense is for the birds . Puerto Ricans like my father were drafted to the Korean War & other wars yet aren’t even eligible to vote for their “ leader aka president “ nor even receive the same federal benefits. NO AMEN till ignorant Americans are educated on basic PR history & why PR remains significantly more impoverished while simultaneously with amazing natural resources. Start learning about the jones act , then explore eugenics, the history & effects of Domino sugar , current tax breaks etc  & keep going but AMEN is not  an appropriate word for Borinquen .
